title: Aegis Portable Hook Intent
description: Schema for hooks/<name>.json — the machine binding (source of truth) for a portable hook intent. The sibling hooks/<name>.md is the human intent doc. The projector (scripts/project.mjs) reads these to generate the Claude .claude-plugin/plugin.json hooks block and the OpenCode compaction region in .opencode/plugins/aegis.js; the HOOK_INTENT validator rule enforces this contract. Hand-edits to the generated host files are validator failures.

intent.description: The portable lifecycle category. Command intents map 1:1 to a host event; the judgment categories 'prompt-type'/'agent-type' are keyed by `name` (D10) and bind to PreToolUse.

name.description: Stable kebab-case identifier, unique across hooks/. Matches the sibling .md frontmatter `name` when a .md exists (D1).

description.description: What the hook does and when it fires.

platforms.description: Non-empty subset of supported hosts this hook projects to.

trigger.description: Optional host-agnostic trigger hints. Host bindings carry the operative matcher.

matcher.description: Host-agnostic matcher hint (e.g. a tool-name alternation).

paths.description: Host-agnostic path globs the hook is scoped to.

xClaude.description: Claude Code binding. Required when `platforms` includes 'claude'.

event.description: Claude hook event (verified against references/claude-code-docs/docs/hooks.md, D2). NOTE: 'UserPromptSubmit' is RESERVED/DEFERRED — it is a valid Claude event kept in this enum for forward-compatibility, but no current hooks/*.json intent binds it. See hooks/AGENTS.md 'Deferred / unimplemented events' for status. Removing it is a wider blast radius than documenting it (v0.0.10 decision: keep + document).

matcher.description: Claude matcher string (e.g. 'startup|clear|compact' for SessionStart, a tool-name alternation for PreToolUse).

dispatch.description: Hook dispatch type. command → a script under .claude-plugin/hooks/; prompt → an inline judgment prompt; agent → an ad-hoc subagent spawned from a prompt (D4). The event→dispatch support table (D3) constrains which dispatch each event allows.

command.description: Repo-relative path to the hook script (required when dispatch is 'command'). The projector prefixes ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/. The tree is FLAT — no subdirectories: projectHooks()'s orphan prune walks .claude-plugin/hooks/ one level deep and raises on a directory rather than descending, so a nested path would name a script the prune cannot account for.

helpers.description: Shared library files under .claude-plugin/hooks/ that this hook sources at runtime (e.g. `source "$(dirname "$0")/lib.sh"`). Declaring a helper is what protects it from projectHooks()'s orphan prune: the keep-set is built by the shared hookTreeKeepSet() in scripts/lib/hook-projection.mjs from command targets plus these declarations, and both the prune and the HOOK_INTENT orphan rule read it. Bare filenames only — the tree is FLAT. This replaces an earlier `_`-prefix naming convention, which protected a file by how it was spelled rather than by any declaration.

prompt.description: Literal judgment prompt (required when dispatch is 'prompt' or 'agent'). Use the $ARGUMENTS placeholder for the hook input JSON (D5). There is NO field to name a pre-defined subagent (D4).

model.description: Optional model override for prompt/agent dispatch.

event.description: OpenCode hook key. This is the LITERAL, flat, dotted property name OpenCode resolves the handler by — verified against the installed @opencode-ai/plugin type contract (dist/index.d.ts, OpenCode 1.18.3), where every one of these is declared as a quoted dotted key on the Hooks interface. A nested object binding (experimental: { session: { compacting } }) declares a different property and is never invoked, so the value here is emitted verbatim as the generated key string.

phase.description: Compaction phase (required when event is 'experimental.session.compacting'). Only 'pre' is expressible: OpenCode's compacting hook fires before compaction starts, and OpenCode exposes no post-compaction hook that can inject context.

handler.description: Name of the handler function in .opencode/plugins/aegis.js the projector wires this binding to.
